program: Business English — Executive Communication for International Leadership
hub_subtitle: Business English Executivo para Reuniões Internacionais e Liderança

lesson.menu_desc: Weighing a business proposal and deciding what to do -- gerund vs infinitive (verb patterns: decide to, suggest -ing, worth -ing, look forward to -ing) -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 13 -- The Proposal -- Deciding What to Do with Verb Patterns
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Izabel Cristina de Pieri | Aula 13 -- The Proposal

listenings.text: Hi Izabel, it is Julian from corporate development, following up on the proposal. Let me lay out the rationale before we decide to move. The idea is simple: I recommend acquiring Nordic Sense, the small sensor startup in Campinas. Their proprietary technology would let us expand into the medical market a full year faster than building it ourselves. That is the upside, and it is significant. Of course, I would avoid rushing. It is worth doing proper due diligence and getting an independent valuation before we commit to anything. The downside, honestly, is integration: merging two engineering cultures always risks slowing both teams down for a quarter or two. My suggestion is that we plan to meet the founders next week, and aim to prepare a short pitch for the board. I really look forward to hearing your read. If you agree it is worth exploring, I will start arranging the first call. Talk soon.

Good morning, Izabel. It is Diana, your CFO, with a quick note on the numbers behind the proposal. I know the board is keen on green-lighting the merger this year, but I would resist committing to a figure too early. First, we cannot afford to skip a proper valuation. Their revenue looks strong, but I want to finish reviewing the last three years before we agree to any ballpark. Second, it is worth remembering that the real cost is not just the price. We would be taking on their debt and their team, so I recommend building a contingency into the model. My advice is to keep the buy-in of the other directors, but to avoid promising a number in the room. Let us aim to present a clean rationale, with upside and downside side by side, and let the board decide. I look forward to walking you through the model on Thursday. Call me if you want to talk it through.
